anna_bhagya.eligible: BPL and AAY (Antyodaya) ration card holders
anna_bhagya.source: anna_bhagya_scheme_document.pdf (scanned image; parameters from Karnataka Govt GO No. FD 01 CSM 2023, dated 10-Jun-2023)

gruha_jyothi.eligible: All domestic consumers with one connection per household
gruha_jyothi.source: gruha_jyothi_scheeme_document.pdf (scanned image; parameters from Karnataka Govt GO No. EN 116 INL 2023, dated 18-Jun-2023)

gruha_lakshmi.eligible: Women head of BPL/APL household (one per family)
gruha_lakshmi.source: Gruha-Lakshmi scheme_document.pdf (scanned image; parameters from Karnataka Govt GO No. WCD 33 WMS 2023, dated 19-Jun-2023)

dm_response.source: Policy Revision DM2020.pdf, Chapter 7: Relief and Rehabilitation, pp. 24-26

flood.source: ActionplanforFloodriskmanagement2021.pdf, Table 2 (pp. 16-18), Chapter 1.8; city risk from Chapter 3 (Bengaluru UFM) and basin flood zonation

trust_mechanics.description: Formal definition of trust variable and its system effects

definition.meaning: Agent's confidence in government institutions, resource delivery reliability, and social safety nets. 1.0 = full trust, 0.0 = no trust.

resource_satisfaction.rule: if resource_received >= 0.8 * resource_demanded: trust += 0.02; else: trust -= 0.05 * (1 - received/demanded)
resource_satisfaction.explanation: Getting <80% of demanded resources erodes trust; adequate supply slowly builds trust

policy_benefit.rule: if agent receives Anna Bhagya/Gruha Jyothi/Gruha Lakshmi benefits: trust += 0.03

shock_event.explanation: Shocks directly reduce trust; magnitude varies by city and shock type

social_influence.explanation: Agent trust converges toward social network average (peer effects)

policy_effectiveness.explanation: Below 50% trust, policy schemes become less effective â€” agents distrust/don't access government programs

cooperation.rule: if trust < 0.4: cooperation_probability *= trust; resource_sharing â†“
cooperation.explanation: Very low trust â†’ agents stop cooperating, hoard resources, refuse collective action

protest_risk.explanation: Below 30% trust â†’ agents may protest, further destabilizing the system (feedback loop)

demand_escalation.explanation: Low trust â†’ agents hoard/stockpile â†’ effective demand increases up to 20% (panic buying effect)

recovery.rule: Each month without shock: trust += 0.01 (capped at initial_value)
recovery.explanation: Trust slowly recovers in stable periods but takes many months to rebuild

trust_mechanics.source: ABM design parameter; thresholds informed by social trust literature (Ostrom 1990, Putnam 2000) and Karnataka post-disaster field reports

Bengaluru.food_supply_note: food_per_hh_kg is PDS ration only (~25-60% of total for poor, ~10% for non-poor). Market supply fills the remainder.
